audience: First-time GitHub user with Python 3.11 or newer.
boundary: Static local assumptions only; no live data, broker API, orders, predictions, portfolio optimization, tax/legal/investment advice, or buy/sell/hold recommendations.
safety_boundaries: Use only local CSV/JSON assumptions or bundled examples.
Do not treat output as tax, legal, investment, or buy/sell/hold advice.
Do not connect this project to broker APIs, live market data, order execution, portfolio optimization, or prediction services.

steps.expected_output: A local folder containing README.md, pyproject.toml, portfolio_fee_drag_simulator/, tests/, and demo/.
steps.title: Get the source

steps.expected_output: The portfolio-fee-drag command is available without installing runtime dependencies.
steps.title: Install locally

steps.expected_output: The command prints wrote lines and ends with quickstart check complete.
steps.title: Generate the deterministic demo

steps.expected_output: A static dashboard, case gallery, and receipt open from local files. On Linux, use xdg-open instead of open.
steps.title: Open the visual artifacts

steps.expected_output: Tests pass, selfcheck returns status pass, and public_scan.json has status pass.
steps.title: Evaluate project health

steps.expected_output: visual_receipt.md, visual_receipt.json, and visual_receipt.html list local routes, bytes, hashes, regeneration commands, and safety boundaries.
steps.title: Know the boundary before sharing
